summary: British high-tech architect (b. 1935), Baron Foster of Thames Bank. Key works: the Gherkin (30 St Mary Axe, London), Reichstag reconstruction (glass dome), HSBC Main Building (Hong Kong), Hearst Tower (Manhattan), Millau Viaduct (world's tallest bridge), Apple Park ('the Spaceship,' Cupertino). Founded Team 4 with Richard Rogers. Known for diagrid facades, glass domes, and sustainable design.

works.name: 30 St Mary Axe ('The Gherkin'), London
works.description: A London skyscraper with a distinctive ovoid/cucumber shape at 30 St Mary Axe in the City of London. Swiss Re is the primary occupant. Built on the ruins of the Baltic Exchange. Features a 'diagrid' structural facade. Known as 'the Gherkin' for its shape.

clues.clue: Ovoid/cucumber-shaped skyscraper at 30 St Mary Axe, London; Swiss Re is primary occupant; known as 'the Gherkin'; built over ruins of the Baltic Exchange
examples: Swiss Re is the primary occupant of a skyscraper he designed at 30 St Mary Axe that is popularly known as 'the Gherkin'
a skyscraper with a distinctive ovoid shape at 30 St Mary Axe
name this British high-tech architect who restored the Reichstag... this British architect who designed a London skyscraper called 'the Gherkin'

works.name: Reichstag Reconstruction (Berlin)
works.description: Following German reunification, Foster added a glass dome to the Reichstag building (Germany's parliament / Bundestag). Inside the dome, a mirror-paneled cone surrounded by two helical ramps reflects sunlight downward. The cone automatically adjusts to control sunlight. Christo and Jean-Claude wrapped the building before the 1990s reconstruction. The building was reconstructed following reunification.

clues.clue: Added glass dome to the Reichstag after German reunification; mirror-paneled cone inside surrounded by two helical ramps (spiral walkway); cone auto-adjusts to control sunlight
examples: Following German reunification, this architect added a steel and glass dome to the Reichstag
He placed a mirror-paneled cone surrounded by two helical ramps under a cupola that he added to a building that was wrapped by Jean-Claude and Christo before its 1990s reconstruction
Double-helix shaped ramps weave up a glass part of this building, whose center contains a mirrored cone that automatically adjusts to control sunlight

clues.clue: Christo and Jean-Claude wrapped the Reichstag before its reconstruction
examples: a building that was wrapped by Jean-Claude and Christo before its 1990s reconstruction

works.name: HSBC Main Building (Hong Kong)
works.description: HSBC headquarters in Hong Kong. The exterior is supported by eight steel masts (freed up the floor space). 'Coathanger' trusses suspend groups of floors ('social villages'). Computer-controlled 'sun scoops' track and reflect sunlight into the atrium. Two cannon-shaped maintenance cranes on the roof are aimed at the bad feng shui from the nearby I.M. Pei Bank of China Tower. A pair of bronze lions at the main entry (replicas of those outside HSBC Shanghai) are touched for good luck. The building's modules were fabricated by Scott Lithgow Shipbuilders in Scotland. A standard example of High-tech Modernism, its exterior displays the steel skeleton.

clues.clue: Exterior supported by eight steel masts; two cannon-shaped cranes on roof aimed at bad feng shui from I.M. Pei's Bank of China Tower
examples: Maintenance cranes looking like 'cannons' are mounted on the roof of a building designed by an architect with this surname; that building is the HSBC Main Building
One of this architect's buildings has two concrete cannons on the roof to combat bad feng shui from a nearby building designed by I.M. Pei
An architect from this country freed up the floor space of a Hong Kong bank by supporting its exterior with eight steel masts

clues.clue: 'Coathanger' trusses suspend floors ('social villages'); computer-controlled 'sun scoops' reflect sunlight into atrium

works.name: Hearst Tower (Manhattan)
works.description: Manhattan skyscraper incorporating a cast-stone base designed by Joseph Urban (1928). Features a 'diagrid' facade with huge concave corner window structures known as 'bird's mouths.'

works.name: Millau Viaduct (France)
works.description: World's tallest bridge, spanning the Tarn River gorge (tributary of the Garonne) in France. Designed with engineer Michel Virlogeux.

clues.clue: World's tallest bridge; spans the Tarn River; designed with engineer Michel Virlogeux

Norman Foster designed this country's Millau Viaduct, the tallest bridge in the world, which crosses over a tributary of its Garonne River

works.name: Apple Park ('The Spaceship'), Cupertino
works.description: Circular office campus in Cupertino, California, nicknamed 'the Spaceship.' Designed with Apple's chief designer Jony Ive. Steve Jobs was heavily involved in the concept. Replaces Apple's 1 Infinite Loop campus. Modeled on the quad at Stanford.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Norman Robert Foster (b. 1935), Baron Foster of Thames Bank. Pioneer of high-tech architecture in Britain. Met Richard Rogers at Yale; together with their future spouses Su Brumwell and Wendy Cheesman, formed Team 4 (designed the Reliance Controls factory). Later founded Foster + Partners (Britain's largest architectural firm). Works in Astana, Kazakhstan: Palace of Peace and Reconciliation (pyramid with 62x62m base, stained glass apex, four 'hands of peace,' inspired by a congress of world religions) and Khan Shatyr Entertainment Center (tent-shaped, inspired by yurts). London City Hall: bulbous 'Glass Gonad' / 'glass testicle' shape to reduce direct sunlight exposure. Masdar City / Masdar (UAE): planned zero-carbon, zero-waste arcological city. Hong Kong International Airport. Wembley Stadium. Torre de Collserola (Barcelona): hollow concrete shaft (4.5m diameter), guy-wire-supported, supports a ten-story pod. Willis Building in London (across from Rogers' Lloyd's Building). Duisburg, Germany redevelopment including zero-emission Eurogate. Won Pritzker Prize 1999. Many Apple stores worldwide also designed by Foster + Partners.

comprehensive_summary:
Norman Robert Foster (b. 1935), Baron Foster of Thames Bank, is a British architect and one of the leading figures of high-tech architecture. He met Richard Rogers at Yale University; together with their future spouses Su Brumwell and Wendy Cheesman, they formed the firm Team 4, which designed the Reliance Controls factory. Foster later founded Foster + Partners, Britain's largest architectural firm.

Foster's most recognizable London building is 30 St Mary Axe, known as 'the Gherkin' for its ovoid/cucumber shape. Swiss Re is the primary occupant. The building, with its diagrid structural facade, was constructed on the ruins of the Baltic Exchange. He also designed the Willis Building in London, which sits across the street from Richard Rogers' Lloyd's Building. Foster's Hearst Tower in Manhattan incorporates a 1928 cast-stone base by Joseph Urban and features a diagrid facade with concave corner windows known as 'bird's mouths.'

Following German reunification, Foster reconstructed the Reichstag in Berlin, adding a glass dome. Inside the dome, a mirror-paneled cone surrounded by two helical ramps reflects and automatically adjusts sunlight downward. Christo and Jean-Claude wrapped the building in polypropylene fabric before the reconstruction.

Foster's HSBC Main Building in Hong Kong is a standard example of High-tech Modernism. Its exterior — which displays its steel skeleton — is supported by eight steel masts that free up the floor space. 'Coathanger' trusses suspend groups of floors the architect called 'social villages.' Computer-controlled 'sun scoops' track and reflect sunlight into the atrium. Two cannon-shaped maintenance cranes on the roof are aimed at the bad feng shui from the nearby I.M. Pei Bank of China Tower. A pair of bronze lions at the main entry — replicas of those outside HSBC Shanghai — are touched for good luck.

Foster designed the Millau Viaduct in France with engineer Michel Virlogeux: the world's tallest bridge, spanning the Tarn River (a tributary of the Garonne). In Cupertino, California, Foster designed the circular Apple Park ('the Spaceship') with Apple's chief designer Jony Ive, replacing Apple's 1 Infinite Loop campus and modeled on the quad at Stanford. Foster + Partners also designed many Apple stores worldwide.

In Astana, Kazakhstan, Foster built the Palace of Peace and Reconciliation — a pyramid with a 62-by-62-meter base, a stained-glass apex, and four 'hands of peace,' inspired by a 2003 Congress of Leaders of World and Traditional Religions — and the Khan Shatyr Entertainment Center (tent-shaped, inspired by yurts, home to the world's largest marquee). London City Hall was built in a bulbous 'Glass Gonad' shape to minimize direct sunlight exposure. Masdar City in Abu Dhabi (UAE) is his planned zero-carbon, zero-waste arcological city. In Barcelona, Foster built the Torre de Collserola — a tower in which a hollow concrete shaft of 4.5 meters in diameter, supported by guy-wires, holds a ten-story pod. He also oversaw a redevelopment of Duisburg, Germany, including the zero-emission Eurogate, and designed the new Wembley Stadium. Foster won the Pritzker Architecture Prize in 1999.
